note: Curated install facts. Mirror packaging/installer.iss + build.ps1 when they change. Not auto-generated.

download.how: Open latest_release and download the asset for your chosen platform/edition. If tools are available, use gh release download or curl to fetch the asset directly.

intake.note: Run BEFORE downloading. Scan the environment, then confirm the two preferences that change what you install and how you configure it. Recommend a default from the scan; don't ask blindly.
environment_scan: OS + arch: Windows, or macOS Apple Silicon vs Intel.
Already installed / running? Existing ~/.bubble-buddy/config.json to preserve?
Network / proxy (affects downloads and the model mirror hf_endpoint).
Does the user have Azure OpenAI access (an endpoint they can sign in to)?

questions.ask: Do you prefer fast cloud transcription (tiny download, best accuracy, needs an Azure account + one browser sign-in, per-use cost) or fully offline/private (no account, free to run, larger download, uses your own CPU/GPU)?
Do you want AI cleanup (polish) of the dictated text, and at what cost?

options.choice: best quality (cloud, needs Azure, extra latency+cost)

recommendation: Has Azure access and wants speed/quality -> Azure edition, backend=azure, polish_engine=azure (polish=auto).
Wants offline/private or has no cloud account -> Full edition, local backend (mlx on Apple Silicon, else faster-whisper), polish_engine=rules or polish=off.
If unsure about polish, default the engine to match the backend: azure backend -> azure polish; local backend -> rules.
Only the azure polish engine needs Azure access; rules/ollama run locally.

editions.label: Full (bundles offline Whisper)
editions.use_when: no cloud account, privacy, fully offline

windows.reinstall_config_note: Default 'Skip' never overwrites an existing config.json (FileExists guard). 'Basic setup' and 'Import' replace it.

install_steps: On first launch, use the Settings panel to pick backend/model/Azure settings.
macos.model_note: The Full DMG includes local inference dependencies, but not model weights. Users choose an MLX model path/repo in Settings; the model downloads on demand.
agent_actions: Use gh release download --repo ThousandOfWind/bubble-buddy --pattern 'BubbleBuddy-Full-*.dmg' for local-model mac installs.
Attach the DMG with hdiutil attach.
Write ~/.bubble-buddy/config.json if the user wants a specific backend/model.
Open /Applications/Bubble Buddy.app and verify it starts.
macos.reinstall_config_note: The packaged app seeds ~/.bubble-buddy/config.json only when missing; existing config is preserved and merged with new missing defaults.

azure_setup.when: Azure edition, or any install where backend or polish_engine is azure.
agent_actions: Ask the user only for their Azure OpenAI endpoint (https://<resource>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/); never ask for an API key.
Write/merge config.json: backend=azure, polish_engine=azure, azure.endpoint=<endpoint>, azure.auth=aad, plus the user's hotkey and language_preference.
Restart the app so it reloads config.
Hand off the sign-in: it needs an interactive browser the user completes; there is no CLI sign-in command.

signin_button.location: A prominent orange banner ABOVE the pet, visible in BOTH the collapsed and expanded states (no need to expand first). Hidden once signed in — not inside a separate Settings dialog.
signin_button.on_success: Overlay shows 'Signed in to Azure'; auth record persists at ~/.bubble-buddy/auth_record.json and survives restarts.

azure_setup.deployments_note: transcribe_deployment defaults to gpt-4o-transcribe and chat_deployment to gpt-4.1; override if the user's Azure resource uses different deployment names.

update.how: Download the latest installer/DMG of the same edition and run it over the existing install.
update.before: Fully quit Bubble Buddy so the executable/app bundle is not locked.
update.preserves_config: Windows: use the default 'Skip' path on the config page to keep config.json; Basic/Import overwrite it. macOS: replacing the app preserves ~/.bubble-buddy/config.json.

uninstall.how: Windows: Settings > Apps, or the Start-menu uninstaller. macOS: quit the app and delete /Applications/Bubble Buddy.app.
uninstall.leftover_user_data: ~/.bubble-buddy is not removed; delete manually for a clean wipe.
